pytorch_transforms.name: RandomResizedCrop
pytorch_transforms.justification: Multi-scale sampling: forces SSL model to understand disease at multiple magnifications. Scale 0.2–1.0 is standard for I-JEPA (Assran et al., 2023).

pytorch_transforms.name: RandomVerticalFlip
pytorch_transforms.justification: Leaf images are captured at arbitrary orientations in field.

pytorch_transforms.name: RandomRotation
pytorch_transforms.justification: Rotation invariance. Limited to ±30° to avoid excessive black padding.

pytorch_transforms.name: ColorJitter
pytorch_transforms.justification: Handles lighting variation. CONSERVATIVE saturation (0.1) and hue (0.05): disease colour IS the signal. Strong colour jitter teaches colour-invariance — opposite of what we need.
pytorch_transforms.risk: MEDIUM if saturation > 0.1 or hue > 0.05. Do not increase these bounds.

pytorch_transforms.name: GaussianBlur
pytorch_transforms.justification: Handles camera focus variation in field images.

pytorch_transforms.name: RandomGrayscale
pytorch_transforms.justification: Forces minimal colour-independent feature learning. 5% only.

pytorch_transforms.name: RandomErasing
pytorch_transforms.justification: Simulates partial occlusion (overlapping leaves in field).

explicitly_excluded: Strong hue shifting (hue > 0.2) — destroys disease colour signal
Elastic deformation — may destroy lesion shape morphology (concentric rings in blight)
CutMix/Mixup — invalid for SSL (no labels to mix)

standard_policy.label: >=10% label fraction

pytorch_transforms.name: RandomResizedCrop
pytorch_transforms.justification: Moderate scale. Retains disease context for correct classification.

pytorch_transforms.name: ColorJitter
pytorch_transforms.justification: Very conservative. Disease colour must be preserved for classification.

excluded: Gaussian blur — may soften lesion boundaries needed for classification
Random grayscale — colour IS the classification signal during fine-tuning

low_label_policy.label: 1%–5% label fraction
low_label_policy.rationale: At 1–5% of 54K images (~380–1,900 images across 38 classes), standard augmentation provides insufficient regularisation. CutMix and Mixup are the most effective label-mixing regularisers for this extreme low-data regime.

additional_transforms.name: CutMix
additional_transforms.justification: Patches crops across images; mixes labels proportionally. Applied at batch level.
additional_transforms.implementation: Apply in training loop, not in Dataset. Use soft cross-entropy loss.

additional_transforms.name: Mixup
additional_transforms.justification: Linear interpolation of image pairs and labels. Complementary to CutMix.

low_label_policy.caveat: CutMix/Mixup may slightly reduce performance at >=25% labels. Use standard policy there.

randaugment_alternative.label: RandAugment (ablation comparison)
randaugment_alternative.rationale: Policy-searched augmentation. Run as ablation vs manual policy. Expected: manual policy better because it explicitly excludes colour-destroying ops.
randaugment_alternative.constraint: MUST exclude: Solarize, Equalize, Posterize, AutoContrast

evaluation.critical_rule: DETERMINISTIC. IDENTICAL across ALL methods. No random transforms.

pytorch_transforms.name: CenterCrop
pytorch_transforms.justification: Deterministic crop. Centre typically contains leaf subject.

explicitly_excluded: Test-Time Augmentation (TTA) in main results — use only as labelled ablation
